item.title: head -n -1 in protect_items_from_groom is not portable on macOS BSD head
item.intent: protect_items_from_groom must work correctly on macOS — replace GNU-only head -n -1 with a portable alternative
steps: Find the head -n -1 call in protect_items_from_groom (line ~2990) used to strip the last line of jq output
Replace head -n -1 with a portable alternative such as: awk 'NR>1{print prev}{prev=$0}' or sed '$d'
Verify the fix correctly strips only the last line (the counts line) while preserving the JSON body
Run shellcheck to confirm no new issues
acceptanceCriteria: protect_items_from_groom produces correct output on both Linux (GNU head) and macOS (BSD head)
The counts line is correctly parsed and the JSON body is correctly written to the live backlog file
